article.headline: Leverkusen leave it late for comeback win over Hoffenheim
article.description: Bundesliga leaders Bayer Leverkusen scored twice late in the game with Robert Andrich and Patrik Schick to earn a 2-1 comeback win over Hoffenheim on Saturday and edge closer to the league title.
article.linkText: Leverkusen late show extends unbeaten run to 39

images.name: Leverkusen's late heroics extends unbeaten run to 39 games
images.caption: Bayer Leverkusen leaves it late to come from a goal down to beat Hoffenheim 2-1 and stay unbeaten this season.

video.headline: Leverkusen's late heroics extends unbeaten run to 39 games
video.caption: Bayer Leverkusen leaves it late to come from a goal down to beat Hoffenheim 2-1 and stay unbeaten this season.
video.title: Leverkusen's late heroics extends unbeaten run to 39 games
video.description: Bayer Leverkusen leaves it late to come from a goal down to beat Hoffenheim 2-1 and stay unbeaten this season.

tracking.trackingName: SOC_One-Play (Leverkusen’s late heroics extends unbeaten run to 39 games) 2024/03/30 ESHEET

article.story:
Bundesliga leaders Bayer Leverkusen scored twice late in the game with Robert Andrich and Patrik Schick to earn a remarkable 2-1 comeback win over Hoffenheim on Saturday and edge closer to the league title.

Leverkusen, unbeaten in 39 matches across all competitions this season, are now on 73 points, 13 ahead of Bayern Munich, who lost 2-0 at home to Borussia Dortmund and remain on 60.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

A day after coach Xabi Alonso announced he would be staying on despite interest from major European clubs, Leverkusen got off to a lacklustre start despite controlling the pace of the game.

Their attacks were stifled and the finishing lacked precision with Hoffenheim's Maximilian Beier silencing the home crowd in the 33rd minute when he broke clear and beat keeper Lukas Hradecky from close range.

"In the first half we had problems to control the game and to reach the final third of the pitch," Alonso said. "But we kept at it and in the second half we scored. It does not matter when. We needed a goal to change the mindset."

The hosts piled on the pressure after the break but were repeatedly denied by keeper Oliver Baumann, including with a spectacular double save in the 76th minute.

With time running out Alonso brought on forward Borja Iglesias, who rattled the crossbar four minutes after entering as the hosts camped in Hoffenheim's box.

The pressure paid off in the 88th minute when Germany international Andrich stabbed in the equaliser from a Florian Wirtz cross.

Schick completed the comeback with a far-post volley in stoppage time, confirming Leverkusen's never-say-die attitude this season.

Alonso's team had come from two goals in both Europa League round-of-16 legs against Qarabag this month to advance 5-4 on aggregate.

"Every game is a huge challenge for us. We were lucky but we showed a very good mentality to keep going, to keep believing. We had a chance to win just like against Qarabag and we did it," Alonso said.

"To have this faith to have a winning mentality, it is something that is created and we have created it this season."

Leverkusen, whose last major title came more than 30 years ago, face Fortuna Dusseldorf in the German Cup semifinal on Tuesday.

With 73 points, they have now equaled their club record from the 1999-2000 campaign.

The result also means that Leverkusen now have the second longest unbeaten run in the history of Europe's top-five leagues, overtaking the 2004-05 Inter Milan team who managed 38 games, and behind only Juventus, who went 42 games without losing in the 2011-12 season.

videos.headline: Hoffenheim stuns Leverkusen with slick opening goal
videos.description: Maximilian Beier finishes off a smart Hoffenheim move to put the visitors 1-0 up on Bundesliga leaders Bayer Leverkusen.

keyEvents.text: Tim Drexler (TSG Hoffenheim) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  Bayer 04 Leverkusen 0, TSG Hoffenheim 1. Maximilian Beier (TSG Hoffenheim) right footed shot from the left side of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Wout Weghorst.
First Half ends, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 0, TSG Hoffenheim 1.
Substitution, TSG Hoffenheim. Grischa Prömel replaces Andrej Kramaric.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Amine Adli replaces Jonas Hofmann.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Borja Iglesias replaces Piero Hincapié.
Substitution, TSG Hoffenheim. Dennis Geiger replaces Umut Tohumcu.
Substitution, TSG Hoffenheim. Ihlas Bebou replaces Maximilian Beier.
Substitution, TSG Hoffenheim. Marius Bülter replaces Wout Weghorst.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Nathan Tella replaces Jeremie Frimpong.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Josip Stanisic replaces Edmond Tapsoba.

Goal!  Bayer 04 Leverkusen 1, TSG Hoffenheim 1. Robert Andrich (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the top left corner. Assisted by Jonathan Tah with a headed pass   following a corner.
Goal!  Bayer 04 Leverkusen 2, TSG Hoffenheim 1. Patrik Schick (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) left footed shot from very close range to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Nathan Tella.
Second Half ends, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 2, TSG Hoffenheim 1.
